data.title: BNP Paribas jumps as target hike provides 'tuning point for investor perception'
data.description:
** Shares in BNP Paribas rise 5.7% to the top of France's blue-chip index CAC 40 , after the French bank raised its mid-term CET1 ratio guidance alongside a 1.15 million euro ($1.32 million) share buyback

** BNP lifts its CET1 ratio target to 13% by 2027, up from 12.5% previously, citing stronger profitability and faster disposal of non-strategic assets

** "BNP is addressing market concerns with raising its CET 1 target to peer level," RBC analysts say, while those at Jefferies add the new target is "a significant turning point for investor perception"

**Shares will see their best day since late March 2022 if gains hold

($1 = 0.8681 euros)

data.title: McBride Shares Rise on Buyback, Potential for Further Returns
data.description:
By Dominic Chopping

McBride shares rose Thursday after the company said it plans to launch a 20 million-pound ($26.1 million) share buyback and seek authorization to expand the buyback program further, saying the current share price undervalues the company.

Shares climbed 11% in early European trade to 122.60 pence and have gained 15% in the year to date.

The supplier of household and personal-care products said Thursday that it expects fiscal-year earnings to land in line with analyst expectations, with continued strength in the overall market for its private label household cleaning products.

Current analyst expectations from a company-compiled consensus point to an adjusted operating profit of 64.3 million pounds for the 12 months to June 30, 2026, McBride said.

"The board continues to have confidence in the group's strategy, leading market position, and financial prospects, and will continue to assess future investment opportunities in that light," it said in a statement.

The company said the current market capitalization significantly undervalues the group, presenting an opportunity to create shareholder value through capital returns.

The 20 million-pound share buyback program will begin shortly, utilizing the maximum allowance of around 10% of outstanding ordinary shares, it said.

McBride added that it would seek shareholder approval to materially expand the buyback authorization from the standard 10% of shares outstanding to up to 25%, once the current authority is close to being exhausted.

In addition, the company said it has loaned the employee benefit trust 7.5 million pounds in October and November 2025 in aggregate to purchase shares for future employee share awards.

The proposed dividend of 3 pence per ordinary share, to be approved later today, provides an additional 5.2 million pound return to shareholders, it added.

Write to Dominic Chopping at dominic.chopping@wsj.com

data.title: Eurozone Bond Yields Tracks U.S. Treasury Yields Higher — Market Talk
data.description: Eurozone government bond yields edge higher, tracking U.S. Treasury yields, as improved market sentiment reduces demand for safe havens such as bonds. Market sentiment improved on the back of Nvidia's results, while the minutes of the Federal Reserve's October meeting lowered rate-cut expectations in December. Eurozone bond markets face significant bond supply from Spain and France on Thursday. The 10-year German Bund yield rises 0.7 basis point to 2.718%; the 10-year Spanish bond yield is up 0.4 basis point at 3.217% and the 10-year French OAT yield is up 0.6 basis point at 3.469%, according to Tradeweb. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

data.title: India's Gretex Corporate Services hits record high after unit plans IPO
data.description:
** Shares of Gretex Corporate Services Ltd rise as much as 3.8% to a record high of 389.4 rupees

** Co says unit Gretex Share Broking proposes to undertake an Initial Public Offering (IPO)

** Adds, IPO may comprise a fresh issue of equity shares and/or an offer for sale

** Stock on track to gain for fifth straight week; up ~1% so far this week

** GREO last up 1.5%; gained ~36% so far since listing on Sept. 4

data.title: CAC 40 Rebounds as Investors Cheer Nvidia Results
data.description:
The CAC 40 rose 1% to 8,045 points on Thursday, snapping a five-session losing streak as investors welcomed Nvidia’s quarterly results, which showed revenue and profits beating expectations.

Sentiment was also shaped by anticipation of the delayed US September jobs report and by the Fed’s latest meeting minutes, which tempered expectations of a December rate cut.

BNP Paribas (+5.8%), Legrand (+2.8%), and Schneider Electric (+2.1%) were among the strongest performers.

Luxury stocks also rebounded from recent declines, with Kering, LVMH, and Hermès all gaining more than 1.5%.

Other notable risers included Teleperformance (+1.6%), Crédit Agricole (+1.5%), and Safran (+1.4%).

data.title: Employment in German auto sector at lowest level in over a decade
data.description:
Employment in Germany's struggling automotive sector is at its lowest level in more than a decade following the elimination of tens of thousands of jobs, said the federal statistics office.

The automotive industry, including parts makers, recorded 721,400 employees at the end of September, the smallest figure since 718,000 in mid-2011, according to data from the office published on Thursday.

As of the end of September, the sector had 48,700 fewer staff than a year earlier, a fall of 6.3% and the biggest drop in any major industrial sector with over 200,000 staff, it said.

By comparison, the manufacturing sector as a whole employed around 5.43 million people at the end of September. This was 120,300, or 2.2%, fewer than a year earlier, said the office.

"The prolonged recession in industry is clearly reflected in employment trends," said Cyrus de la Rubia, chief economist at Hamburg Commercial Bank.

Nevertheless, the car industry remains Germany's second-largest manufacturing sector in terms of employment, after mechanical engineering, which counts around 934,200 people as staff.

German manufacturers are struggling with high U.S. tariffs, the rise of Chinese electric vehicle makers and, most recently, chip supply difficulties due to a dispute over Nexperia.

Nevertheless, the mood in the German automotive industry improved noticeably in October, according to a survey by the Ifo economic institute earlier this month. The sector's business climate index rose to minus 12.9 points from minus 21.3 points in September, it found.

data.title: ELABS: Q3 revenue surged 50% with strong laptop contracts and a strategic review underway
data.description:
Q3 revenue rose 50% year-over-year to NOK 42.3 million, driven by milestone laptop contracts, with EBITDA up to NOK 12.7 million. High shipment volumes and a growing install base set the stage for future pay-as-you-go revenue, while a strategic review is underway to refine commercial priorities.

data.title: ZAWYA: Kuwait Petroleum Corporation secures $4.87bln syndicated facility
data.description:
Staff Writer

Kuwait Petroleum Corporation (KPC) has secured a KD1.5 billion ($4.87 billion) syndicated facility from Kuwait Finance House (KFH) to fund the state-owned oil company’s capital expenditure programme.

KFH has led the Islamic tranche valued at KD 675 million ($2.19 billion) and will manage its syndication. The bank will also act as the Islamic Investment Agent for the transaction.

KFH’s individual participation in the facility amounts to KD 405 million ($1.3 billion).

(Writing by Farah Heiba, Editing by Daniel Luiz)

Farah.heiba@lseg.com

data.title: Eramls Investment Alliance shaped by Nolan Mercer Debuts Documentary on Its Responsibility-Driven Identity
data.description:
Eramls Investment Alliance, shaped by the vision of Nolan Mercer, debuts a new documentary exploring the evolution of its responsibility-centered brand identity and its enduring commitment to human-focused values, ethical purpose, and cultural transformation.

NEW YORK CITY, NY / ACCESS Newswire / November 20, 2025 / Eramls Investment Alliance announced the debut of a new documentary film examining the organization's responsibility-driven identity and the human-centered philosophy that has guided its cultural development over the past two decades. Shaped by the vision of Founder and Chief Executive Nolan Mercer, the film captures the evolution of Eramls as a purpose-led institution grounded in empathy, responsibility, and long-term societal value.

<img height="0" src="https://app.accessnewswire.com/api/iframe.ashx?releaseid=1105075&url=https%3a%2f%2fwww.youtube.com%2fembed%2fbmqMpiiX5uo" width="0"/></div></div></div><p>The documentary serves as a cinematic representation of Eramls' foundational belief that meaningful progress must be rooted in ethical intention and a commitment to humanity. Through a combination of archival footage, interviews, narrative storytelling, and visual symbolism, the film highlights how the organization's brand identity has matured in parallel with its growing focus on responsible innovation, transparent leadership, and global cultural impact.</p><p>According to Nolan Mercer, the documentary reflects not only the path Eramls has taken, but also the principles that continue to shape its future.<br/>"Identity is not built through ambition alone; it is built through purpose," Mercer said. "This film captures the essence of what Eramls represents - a commitment to love, responsibility, and the idea that progress must serve people first."</p><p>The documentary traces several pivotal moments in Eramls' brand history, including its transition from a traditional financial organization into a multidisciplinary platform integrating technology, education, and sustainability. It explores the values that form the backbone of the Eramls philosophy - ethical awareness, cultural inclusivity, and a belief in the transformative potential of responsible leadership.</p><p>Key themes featured in the film include:</p><p>1. The Evolution of Responsibility as a Brand Foundation</p><p>The film showcases how responsibility became the defining element of the Eramls identity, influencing strategic decisions, organizational culture, and public engagement.</p><p>2. Human-Centered Innovation</p><p>Through interviews with internal teams, creative partners, and cultural analysts, the documentary highlights Eramls' focus on designing systems, programs, and experiences that prioritize human development and ethical intent.</p><p>3. Global Cultural Integration</p><p>Audiences are offered a look into how Eramls integrated diverse cultural narratives, global perspectives, and shared values into its brand expression, ensuring that its message resonates across regions and generations.</p><p>4. The Enduring Influence of Nolan Mercer's Vision</p><p>The documentary underscores Mercer's role in shaping a brand philosophy grounded in empathy, responsibility, and moral clarity - principles that continue to define the organization's direction.</p><p>The debut of the documentary marks a significant milestone in Eramls Investment Alliance's ongoing effort to articulate and preserve its cultural identity. The film will be showcased across multiple digital platforms, with future screenings planned in collaboration with educational institutions, cultural organizations, and global partners focused on responsible innovation and human development.</p><p>Eramls spokesperson Courtney Blake stated, "This documentary is more than a film - it is a reflection of the values that have guided Eramls from its earliest days. By sharing this story, the organization reaffirms its dedication to fostering a purpose-driven brand that stands for integrity, compassion, and ethical progress."</p><a href="https://app.accessnewswire.com/imagelibrary/c77c21eb-156c-4226-acf3-da2f89fa2a6e/14.jpg" rel="nofollow"><img src="https://app.accessnewswire.com/imagelibrary/c77c21eb-156c-4226-acf3-da2f89fa2a6e/1105075/14.jpg" style="width:705px;text-align:center;margin:0.9em auto;"/></a><div style="clear: both"/><p>About Eramls Investment Alliance</p><p>Founded by Nolan Mercer, Eramls Investment Alliance is a global organization dedicated to ethical innovation, cultural development, and responsible leadership. Through initiatives rooted in transparency, education, and human-centered design, Eramls advances its mission of shaping a future where progress is aligned with purpose and responsibility.</p><p>Company Name: Eramls<br/>Contact Person: Courtney Blake<br/>Email: <a href="mailto:service@eramls.com" rel="nofollow">service@eramls.com</a><br/>Website: <a href="https://pr.report/geo8" rel="nofollow">https://eramls.com/</a></p><p><strong>SOURCE:</strong> Eramls</p><br/><br/>View the original <a href="https://www.accessnewswire.com/newsroom/en/banking-and-financial-services/eramls-investment-alliance-shaped-by-nolan-mercer-debuts-documentary-1105075">press release</a> on ACCESS Newswire<br/><br/><img height="0" src="https://app.accessnewswire.com/img.ashx?id=1105075" width="0"/></body></html>

data.title: Schott Pharma climbs after Berenberg upgrades to 'buy'
data.description:
** Shares in Schott Pharma are up around 2.5% after Berenberg raised its rating on German pharmaceutical industry supplier to "buy" from "hold"

** The broker says the company is set to benefit from recovering drug containment demand

** Superiority of GLP-1 injectable diabetes drugs, structural trends in biologics and ready-to-use vials underpin sustainable long-term market growth, with peers projecting restocking and higher production volumes into 2026, it notes

** After having peaked in 2023, the broker expects Schott Pharma's capital expenditures to normalise at around 12-13% of sales in 2026-2027, positioning the company for stable free cash flow generation from 2026 onwards

** The broker adds it sees the company's valuation as "attractive"

** Out of 13 analysts that cover Schott Pharma, 11 rate the stock "strong buy" or "buy," ​one rates it "hold" and one "sell" - LSEG data
